content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. 3OX 307 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement
(916) 322-5662          322-5660             322-5901              322-6441

AUGUST 29, 1984


P. Lawrence Klose
City Attorney
City of Davis
23 Russell Blvd.
Davis, CA  95616

                                        Re: Your Request for Advice
                                            Our No. A-84-219

Dear Mr. Klose:

     You have requested advice on behalf of City of Davis Design
Review Commissioner Paul Hart. The facts which you have
provided to us are as follows:

                                FACTS

     As part of their duties, the Design Review
Commissioners of the City of Davis review and approve
trash container size, location, number and design as
well as overall site design layout for refuse
collection facilities. A Commission decision could
have a considerable impact on the cost-effectiveness of
collection and therefore, indirectly, on the income of
the Davis Waste Removal Company, the City's exclusive
refuse collection contractor. Whether a decision had a
"material financial effect" on the Company would depend
on the volume of service affected.

     A recently appointed Commissioner, Paul Hart, is an
officer and 50% shareholder of the Davis Waste Removal
Company. He is clearly within the financial interest
provisions of Government Code section 87103 (c) and
(d).

                                QUESTIONS

     You have posed the following questions:

P. Lawrence Klose
August 29, 1984
Page 2


     1)   Does his position on the Design Review Commission
          present potential conflict of interest violations
          by Mr. Hart?

     2)   If so, how should the situation best be addressed?

                              ANALYSIS

     Your letter has stipulated that Commissioner Hart has a
financial interest in the Davis Waste Removal Company within the
meaning of Government Code Section 87103.1/  Therefore, he is
required to disqualify himself from making, participating in
making, or using his official position to influence any Design
Review Commission decision which will have a material financial
effect upon the Company.  Government Code Sections 87100, 87103,
2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18702.2/  (Copy enclosed.)
Consequently, the answer to your first question is yes.

     The answer to your second question is found in 2 Cal. Adm.
Code Section 18700(b)(5).  (Copy enclosed.)  This provides the
methodology for declaring that he is disqualifying himself from
participation in such decisions.  It provides as follows:

          (b)   A public official "makes a governmental
     decision," except as provided in subsection (d) of this
     section, when he or she, acting within the authority of
     his or her office:

                              * * *

          (5)   Determines not to act, within the meaning of
     sub-paragraphs (1), (2), (3) or (4), unless such
     determination is made because of his or her financial
     interest.  When the determination not to act occurs
     because of his or her financial interest, the
     official's determination must be accompanied by
     disclosure of the financial interest, made part of the
     agency's official record or made in writing to the

_________________________

     1/  Your letter states that he has a financial interest
under 37103(c) and (d).  However, as a 50% shareholder, he
presumably also has a financial interest under Section 37103(a).

     2/  See specifically subdivisions (b)(1) and (b)(3)(A) and
(C).

P. Lawrence Klose
August 29, 1984
Page 3


     official's supervisor, appointing power or any other
person specified in a conflict of interest code adopted
pursuant to Government Code Section 87300. (Emphasis
added.)

     Whenever Mr. Hart is presented with a Commission decision
which he knows (or has reason to know) will have a material
financial effect upon his company, then he should immediately
take the necessary steps so to remove himself entirely from the
decision-making process.

     If you have any further questions, please do not hesitate to
contact me at (916) 322-5901.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Robert E. Leidigh
                                        Counsel
                                        Legal Division

REL:plh
Enclosures

DEPARTMENTS:
□ COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT 916-756-3746
□ FISCAL AND INFORMATION SERVICES 756-3742
□ FIRE 756-3743
□ LEGAL SERVICES 756-3744
□ MANAGEMENT SERVICES 756-3745
□ PARKS AND COMMUNITY SERVICES 756-3747
□ POLICE 756-3740
□ PUBLIC WORKS 756-3749

CITY OF DAVIS 23 Russell Blvd. Davis, CA 95616

August 21, 1984

Ms. Barbara A. Milman
Chief Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Potential Conflict of Interest
Design Review Commission

Dear Ms. Milman:

As part of their duties, the Design Review Commissioners of
the City of Davis review and approve trash container size,
location, number and design as well as overall site design
layout for refuse collection facilities. A Commission
decision could have a considerable impact on the
cost-effectiveness of collection and therefore, indirectly,
on the income of the Davis Waste Removal Company, the City's
exclusive refuse collection contractor. Whether a decision
had a "material financial effect" on the Company would
depend on the volume of service affected.

A recently appointed Commissioner, Paul Hart, is an officer
and 50% shareholder of the Davis Waste Removal Company. He
is clearly within the financial interest provisions of
Government Code section 87103 (c) and (d). We would
appreciate your response to two questions:

1) Does his position on the Design Review Commission
present potential conflict of interest violations
by Mr. Hart?

2) If so, how should the situation best be addressed?

Very truly yours,

P. LAWRENCE KLOSE
City Attorney

PLK:dk

sections.question:
You have posed the following questions: 


P. Lawrence Klose 
August 29, 1984  
1) 
Does his position on the Design Review Commission 
present potential conflict of interest violations 
by Mr. Hart? 
2) 
If so, how should the situation best be addressed?
sections.conclusion: Your letter has stipulated that Commissioner Hart has a financial interest in the Davis Waste Removal Company within the meaning of Government Code Section 87103. Therefore, he is required to disqualify himself from making, participating in making, or using his official position to influence any Design Review Commission decision which will have a material financial effect upon the Company. Consequently, the answer to your first question is yes. The answer to your second question is found in 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18700 (b) (5), which provides the methodology for declaring that he is disqualifying himself from participation in such decisions through disclosure of the financial interest made part of the agency's official record.

sections.question_synthetic: Whether a Design Review Commissioner who is a 50% shareholder and officer of the city's exclusive refuse collection contractor has a conflict of interest when the commission makes decisions affecting trash container design and site layout that could materially impact the contractor's income, and if so, how to address it.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Yes, Commissioner Hart has a conflict of interest and must disqualify himself from any Design Review Commission decision that would have a material financial effect on Davis Waste Removal Company. He must do so by formally disclosing his financial interest and removing himself entirely from the decision-making process, with the disclosure made part of the agency's official record.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: modern; LLM: This is a clear FPPC advice letter (A-84-219) responding to a request from City Attorney P. Lawrence Klose. The document includes both the incoming request (August 21, 1984) and FPPC's response (August 29, 1984). Both questions and conclusions are clearly marked. Minor confidence deduction due to footnote references that provide additional technical detail not fully captured.

QUESTION: You have posed the following questions: 


P. Lawrence Klose 
August 29, 1984  
1) 
Does his position on the Design Review Commission 
present potential conflict of interest violations 
by Mr. Hart? 
2) 
If so, how should the situation best be addressed?

CONCLUSION: Your letter has stipulated that Commissioner Hart has a financial interest in the Davis Waste Removal Company within the meaning of Government Code Section 87103. Therefore, he is required to disqualify himself from making, participating in making, or using his official position to influence any Design Review Commission decision which will have a material financial effect upon the Company. Consequently, the answer to your first question is yes. The answer to your second question is found in 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18700 (b) (5), which provides the methodology for declaring that he is disqualifying himself from participation in such decisions through disclosure of the financial interest made part of the agency's official record.

embedding.summary: FPPC advises the City of Davis that Design Review Commissioner Paul Hart, who is a 50% shareholder and officer of the city's exclusive refuse collection contractor, must disqualify himself from commission decisions affecting trash container design and refuse collection facilities that could materially impact the contractor's income. The disqualification must be accomplished through formal disclosure of the financial interest as part of the official record.
